description: Skill-driven workflow library (#287). Canonical catalog of substrate workflows that Claude (and other AI agents) can invoke via the MCP typed-tool surface (#284). Each skill is a named, parameterizable procedure with declared preconditions, side effects, and success criteria. Mirrors the page-types library (#250) but for substrate OPERATIONS rather than CONTENT.

skills.name: Resolve a uniqueness_gate / mood_lock / pattern_entropy finding
preconditions: forge.toml has [site_identity] declared
Most recent forge build produced strict findings citing var-* or pattern-* or theme-*
steps: Run `forge build` to capture current findings.
For each variation-arc finding (var-001..var-002, pattern-001..pattern-502, theme-101..theme-103, mood-001..mood-002, ident-201..ident-206): read the advocacy.fix line.
Edit cms/*.json or forge.toml per the substrate-fix; never disable the gate.
Re-run `forge build`. Iterate until findings clear OR the gate's threshold is raised in forge.toml with operator approval.
skills.success_criteria: forge build returns 0 strict findings on the variation arc.

skills.name: Atomic identity change
preconditions: Operator has decided to change one or more [site_identity] axes
reports/attest-key.b64 exists (sign the transition record)
steps: Snapshot current identity via `forge identity show --json`. Save the identity_hash.
Edit forge.toml [site_identity] axis(es) to the new value(s).
Run `forge identity show --json` again. Confirm new identity_hash differs.

Call forge_core::identity_transition::classify_diff(prev, next). Confirm IdentityDiff::is_atomic() returns true. If not, add the missing cascade-partner change (e.g. voice.tier change requires allowed/forbidden primitives change).
Construct an IdentityTransition record, sign with reports/attest-key.b64, append to reports/identity-transitions.jsonl.
Run `forge build`. Iterate on identity_coherence findings until clean.
skills.success_criteria: forge build passes + transition signature verifies.

skills.name: Add a new substrate primitive to Loom
preconditions: Working tree on PlausiDen-Loom is clean
The new primitive has been justified per [[substrate-only-path]] (gap in existing vocabulary, not a workaround)
steps: Add a new CmsSection variant to loom-cms-render with serde rename_all="snake_case".
Add a corresponding Maud template in loom-cms-render/src/primitives/<name>.rs.
Add CSS for the primitive in loom-skin/src/primitives/<name>.css.
Bump loom-cms-render's primitive_count constant.
Run cargo test -p loom-cms-render and confirm new tests pass.
Update docs/traits-catalog.md with the new primitive's mood-affinity classification.
Run cargo test from /home/paul/projects/PlausiDen-Forge to confirm Forge can render the new primitive (loom-sync phase pulls the latest).
side_effects: may modify forge-phases mood_lock affinity tables
skills.success_criteria: primitive renders in Loom + passes Forge build with [editorial_purity] enforce=true.

skills.name: Author CMS content for a new page
preconditions: forge.toml [site_identity] declared
Target content_type slug exists in [[site_identity.content_type]]
Page-type template selected from corpora/page_types.json
steps: Pick a template from corpora/page_types.json matching the target content_type.
Create cms/<slug>.json with the template's section_signature filled in.
Populate text fields (title, body, lede) — keep voice within the declared [site_identity.voice].tier.
Run `forge build`. Iterate on findings from voice_profile_audit (#241), zone_constraints (#254), pattern_entropy (#236), composition_lineage (#244).
skills.success_criteria: forge build passes for the new page.

skills.name: Register a fingerprint to the platform registry
preconditions: forge build produces 0 strict findings
reports/attest-key.b64 exists
Operator confirms this build represents the canonical state of the site
steps: Compute the current SiteFingerprint via forge_core::fingerprint (walks cms/*.json).
Open reports/attest-key.b64 to load the Ed25519 SigningKey.
Call forge_core::fingerprint_registry::append(path, site_id, tenant_id, fingerprint, timestamp, &key).
Run `forge fingerprint verify` to confirm chain integrity after the append.
skills.success_criteria: fingerprint append succeeds + chain verify passes.

skills.name: Synthesize a site spec from a reference URL
preconditions: Crawler binary built + Loom + Forge built
Reference URL accessible from the build host
steps: Invoke crawler.capture(url, viewports=[390,768,1280]). Saves to capture_dir.
Run per-axis extractors (#264-#272) against capture_dir. Each emits its structured AxisResult.
Pipe AxisResults through forge_core::reference_match::map() to produce a spec.
Show spec preview to operator; iterate on token/primitive/voice overrides.
Run forge_core::reference_match::synthesize() to emit cms/*.json + forge.toml updates.
Run `forge build`. Iterate on variation-arc findings until clean.
skills.success_criteria: forge build passes + visual diff vs reference at 3 viewports within operator-acceptable threshold.

skills.name: Rotate the attestation Ed25519 keypair
preconditions: Operator decides to rotate (e.g. key suspected compromised, scheduled rotation)
Existing reports/attest-pubkey.b64 backed up if downstream verifiers pin it
steps: Backup existing reports/attest-key.b64 + reports/attest-pubkey.b64 with timestamped suffix.
Run `forge attest init --force` to generate new keypair.
Sign a 'rotation-witness' record into reports/identity-transitions.jsonl noting the rotation event.
Publish new pubkey to downstream verifiers + update plausiden.com /.well-known/pubkey.
Run `forge fingerprint verify --signatures` with old pubkey to confirm prior chain still verifies (signatures from before rotation use old key).
side_effects: publishes new pubkey to public well-known path
skills.success_criteria: new pubkey works for fresh signs + old pubkey verifies prior chain entries.

skills.name: Add or revise a doctrine rule
preconditions: Working tree on PlausiDen-AVP-Doctrine is clean
Operator has identified a substrate behavior that should be enforced or relaxed
steps: Open PlausiDen-AVP-Doctrine/standing-orders.
Pick a stable rule_id namespace (prim-NNN, sec-NNN, a11y-NNN, etc.).
Write rule_id + statement + rationale + enforcement (the SUBSTRATE_ERRORS.md triple shape).
Cross-reference from any Forge phases that should cite this rule via .citing([rule_id]).
Commit + push.
Run `forge doctrine query --rule <id>` to confirm the rule resolves.
skills.success_criteria: doctrine query resolves; findings citing the rule render the rationale.

skills.name: Pixel reproduction loop for a reference site
preconditions: Crawler + Forge + firefox-esr installed
Target site URL is in corpora/reference_baseline.json
steps: Use reference-match skill to generate initial cms/ + forge.toml.
Run `forge build`. Deploy to /var/www/dev.plausiden.com (rsync from PlausiDen-Forge/static/).
Screenshot reference site at 390/768/1280 px via Crawler.
Screenshot dev.plausiden.com at the same viewports via firefox-esr --headless.
Diff visually (operator review or perceptual-hash distance).
Identify gaps: missing primitives, off-palette tokens, wrong density.
Add missing primitives to Loom (substrate, not site-specific). Adjust tokens. Iterate.
When perceptual match is within operator-acceptable threshold, register fingerprint.
skills.success_criteria: perceptual diff < operator threshold at all 3 viewports AND forge build passes.

skills.name: Triage the substrate backlog
preconditions: Backlog has 30+ pending tasks
steps: Run TaskList (or equivalent).
Classify each task per docs/ARCHITECTURAL_THREADS.md: shipped / in-progress / pending-load-bearing / pending-enumerative / pending-speculative.
Mark `status=deleted` for subsumed tasks.
Mark `status=deleted` for speculative tasks that block on un-shipped infrastructure (file a follow-on note when the dependency lands).
Re-classify cross-arc tasks to their correct arc.
Update docs/ARCHITECTURAL_THREADS.md to reflect the cleanup.
skills.success_criteria: active task count reflects real remaining substrate work, not enumerative dumps.

notes: Categories: variation-arc / controlled-mutability / substrate-extension / content-authoring / reference-matching / attestation / doctrine / meta.
Each skill's steps list is the canonical procedure. Variations are operator discretion; substantive deviations should propose a new skill_slug rather than mutating the canonical one.
Skills MUST cite the doctrine docs they implement via doctrine_refs. Future #279 generality-assessment ensures every skill's procedure is generic across tenants.

Per [[backward-compat-version-discipline]]: skill_slug + category + preconditions + side_effects + success_criteria is the wire shape. Renaming a slug breaks consumer scripts; deprecate via removal with 1-version overlap.
Future MCP server impl invokes skills via the `skills.invoke` tool (#284) and records each invocation via skill_telemetry (#278).
